Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The normative process defines Sufficient inventory path Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order Insufficient inventory path Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key constraints 1 Process always begins with Receive order 2 Check inventory must occur before any decisions about payment authorization or fulfillment 3 Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging 4 Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping 5 Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation 6 Close order should be the final activity in all scenarios 7 After cancellation no fulfillment activities packaging shipping should occur 8 Cancellation path after checking inventory directly proceed to Cancel order implying no authorization of payment in the cancellation path

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Receive Check Authorize Package Ship Close This matches the sufficient inventory path exactly COMPLIANT

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Receive Check Package Authorize Ship Close Package items occurs BEFORE Authorize payment Violation Package items should only occur after authorization payment authorization must come before packaging Also Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging here it is after packaging VIOLATION

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Receive Check Authorize Cancel Ship Close Multiple issues Cancel order after Authorize payment in the cancellation path after checking inventory the process should directly proceed to Cancel order Authorization happening before cancel suggests a mixed path The description says the cancellation scenario should be Receive Check Cancel Close So Authorize payment before Cancel deviates from this Also arguably payment should not be authorized for an order that gets canceled Ship order AFTER Cancel order explicit violation Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation Also Once an order is canceled it would be illogical to continue with activities intended for fulfillment such as packaging or shipping Also Ship order without Package items there is no Package items activity at all in this case so shipping occurs without packaging which violates Ship order should only occur after packaging VIOLATION multiple

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive Check Cancel Close Matches the insufficient inventory path exactly COMPLIANT

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Receive Check Authorize Ship Close Missing Package items Ship order occurs without prior packaging Violation Ship order should only occur after packaging Is skipping Package items a violation The expected sequence is Receive Check Authorize Payment Package Ship Close Skipping Package means Ship does not follow packaging Yes VIOLATION

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3s Authorize before Cancel The description says Check inventory step must occur before any decisions about payment authorization or fulfillment are made Authorization did occur after check so that constraint is satisfied But the cancellation path says after checking the inventory the process should directly proceed to Cancel order The word directly suggests no intermediate activities So Authorize payment between Check and Cancel is a deviation Also logically authorizing payment for an order that will be canceled is problematic

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: One might debate whether skipping an activity is a violation or just an incomplete recording but per the description Ship order should only occur after packaging so shipping without packaging violates the precedence constraint

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Compliant cases 1 and 4 Deviating cases 2 3 and 5
